name: Greg Osby
abstract: Greg Osby (born August 3, 1960) is an American jazz saxophonist and composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: With Scott and Greg Osby, and it was supposed to be a concert. Just, just the two of us… three of us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Greg Osby, who came to the band... Greg Osby had gone from the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: it had Mick Goodrick on guitar, let’s see, Lonnie Plaxico on bass and Greg Osby on alto and Gary Thomas on tenor.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: It was Regina and Greg Osby. Mino Cinelu played drums. ... That started our relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: And I think these days, she’s companion… she has a companion, Greg Osby.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
